target.name: Porotech
target.founder: Tongtong Zhu, Yingjun Liu, Rachel Oliver

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Porotech is a Series A-stage semiconductor company that has raised $20M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a peer set of four same-stage, same-sector private companies (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) valued between $500M and $5.0B, plus three public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) with a median price-to-sales multiple of 22.3x.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $20M raised proxy, implying a $40M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the four private comparables yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as Porotech's revenue is not disclosed. These two methods triangulate to a range of $34M to $1.7B, with a median of $770M.

The wide valuation spread reflects material uncertainty around Porotech's competitive position within the frontier chip market relative to better-capitalized peers. Stage-progression suggests a floor near $34M based on capital deployed, while peer-median suggests meaningful upside if Porotech's technology and market access approach the upper quartile of the peer set. Resolution of product-market fit and customer traction would narrow this range materially.
